Purpose: Horizontal infographic banner showing hopper's per-item workflow — CLAIM, PLAN, EXECUTE, VALIDATE, COMMIT, INTEGRATE — with the Anthropic profile's model assignments labeled at the three engineering phases. Editorial style suitable for a clean light-themed blog. No cyberpunk, no dark backgrounds, no glow effects, no character.

Style.Genre: Clean editorial infographic, like a magazine or technical blog diagram
Style.Rendering: Flat vector-style illustration with subtle paper-grain texture, clean lines, soft shadows. NOT cyberpunk, NOT photorealistic, NOT painterly.

Lighting.Type: Flat ambient, no dramatic light sources, no glow
Lighting.Shadows: Very subtle soft drop-shadows under panels for gentle depth — no high contrast

Primary: Soft slate blue (#007acc) for outlines and the title
Accents: Warm amber/orange (#f59e0b) reserved for the Anthropic model badges
Light cool grey (#9ca3af) for harness-action labels
ColorPalette.Treatment: Mostly white with thin coloured outlines. Amber appears only on the three model badges so it reads as the editorial focal accent. Everything else is restrained black-text-on-white.

Effects: No glow, no neon, no scanlines, no holographic effects
Flat clean shapes with thin 1–2px outlines
Subtle soft drop shadows for depth
Atmosphere.Mood: Calm, clean, editorial — fits a serious technical blog post, not a sci-fi poster

PromptKeywords: minimal colour palette — black text on white with soft blue outlines and amber accent badges
CRITICAL: every text label must be spelled exactly, no letter substitutions, no missing or doubled letters

exact required spellings: CLAIM, PLAN, EXECUTE, VALIDATE, COMMIT, INTEGRATE, Claude Opus 4.7, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Anthropic, next item, harness action, Model Invocation, Harness Action, Hopper, Engineering Sequence

CompositionNotes.Background: Pure white #ffffff across the full canvas — no gradient, no texture, no border
CompositionNotes.Framing: Pure flat-illustration infographic, no human figure, generous white margins around the pipeline so it breathes
CompositionNotes.Layout: Six equally-sized rounded-rectangle node panels arranged in a single row, left-to-right, with comfortable spacing. Title line above the row. Slim legend at the bottom. A thin dashed grey loop-back arrow underneath returning from INTEGRATE to CLAIM with a small 'next item' label.
CompositionNotes.DepthOfField: Flat, no depth of field, all elements crisp
CompositionNotes.TypographyTreatment: Clean sans-serif (something like Inter, Helvetica, or system-ui). Phase names in medium-weight all-caps. Model badge text in regular weight, mixed case. Title in semibold.

Situation.Name: Hopper Per-Item Workflow — Anthropic Profile (editorial)
Situation.Setting: Pure white canvas. No environment, no scene, no atmosphere. This is a flat illustrated diagram, like one you'd find in a clean technical blog post.

Article.Name: Title
Article.Description: Plain text near the top reading 'Hopper — Engineering Sequence' in a clean semibold sans-serif. Smaller subtitle line below in regular weight, soft grey: 'Anthropic profile model assignments'. Left-aligned or centered above the pipeline row.
Article.Application: Top of frame, simple text only — no panel, no border around the title
Article.SymbolicMeaning: Sets the diagram as a hopper-specific pipeline view

Article.Name: Stage 1 — CLAIM
Article.Description: Rounded-rectangle node with a thin light-grey outline (1–2px), white fill, the word 'CLAIM' in dark charcoal all-caps, and a small line-icon (queue/ticket) above the word. No amber badge — instead a single line of small grey text beneath the panel reads 'harness action'.
Article.Application: Leftmost of the six nodes; visually de-emphasized so it's clear no model is invoked here
Article.SymbolicMeaning: Worker claims the next queued item

Article.Name: Stage 2 — PLAN
Article.Description: Rounded-rectangle node with a slightly stronger soft-blue outline (#007acc, 1.5px), white fill, the word 'PLAN' in dark charcoal all-caps, and a small clipboard line-icon. Below the panel, a small amber pill badge (warm amber #f59e0b fill, white text) reads 'Claude Opus 4.7'.
Article.Application: Second from left; the blue outline plus amber badge signal this is an engineering phase with a model invocation
Article.SymbolicMeaning: Engineering phase — planning, executed by Opus 4.7

Article.Name: Stage 3 — EXECUTE
Article.Description: Rounded-rectangle node, matching style to PLAN: blue outline, white fill, 'EXECUTE' in dark charcoal, a small gear or code-bracket line-icon. Below, an amber pill badge reads 'Claude Sonnet 4.6'.
Article.Application: Third from left; identical visual treatment to PLAN and VALIDATE so the three engineering phases read as a group
Article.SymbolicMeaning: Engineering phase — execution, handled by Sonnet 4.6

Article.Name: Stage 4 — VALIDATE
Article.Description: Rounded-rectangle node matching PLAN/EXECUTE style: blue outline, white fill, 'VALIDATE' in dark charcoal, a small checkmark-shield line-icon. Below, an amber pill badge reads 'Claude Opus 4.7'.
Article.Application: Fourth from left; same model-invocation visual treatment
Article.SymbolicMeaning: Engineering phase — validation, back to Opus 4.7

Article.Name: Stage 5 — COMMIT
Article.Description: Rounded-rectangle node matching CLAIM style: thin light-grey outline, white fill, 'COMMIT' in dark charcoal, a small git-commit line-icon. Below, small grey text 'harness action'.
Article.Application: Fifth from left; visually de-emphasized like CLAIM
Article.SymbolicMeaning: Hopper auto-commits worktree changes — no LLM

Article.Name: Stage 6 — INTEGRATE
Article.Description: Rounded-rectangle node matching CLAIM/COMMIT style: thin light-grey outline, white fill, 'INTEGRATE' in dark charcoal, a small git-merge line-icon. Below, small grey text 'harness action'.
Article.Application: Rightmost of the six nodes; de-emphasized like CLAIM and COMMIT
Article.SymbolicMeaning: Hopper merges worktree branch to main and cleans up

Article.Name: Rightward connector arrows
Article.Description: Five thin solid grey rightward chevron arrows linking each stage to the next. No glow, no gradient, just clean simple arrows.
Article.Application: Horizontal arrows between the six nodes — CLAIM→PLAN, PLAN→EXECUTE, EXECUTE→VALIDATE, VALIDATE→COMMIT, COMMIT→INTEGRATE

Article.Name: Loop-back hint
Article.Description: A single thin dashed light-grey arrow curving from the right side (under INTEGRATE) back to the left side (under CLAIM) along the bottom edge of the row, with a tiny grey 'next item' label positioned along the curve
Article.Application: Bottom of the diagram, below the node row, very subtle
Article.SymbolicMeaning: Worker loops back to claim the next queued item

Article.Name: Legend
Article.Description: Slim line at the very bottom showing two small color swatches with labels in small grey text: amber pill = 'Model Invocation' and small grey square = 'Harness Action'
Article.Application: Very bottom of frame, low-key reference
Article.SymbolicMeaning: Reader can decode badge colors at a glance

KeyDetails: Background is pure white — no gradient, no texture, no border, no glow
Six rounded-rectangle stage nodes arranged in a single horizontal row with generous white margins around them

Three engineering phases (PLAN, EXECUTE, VALIDATE) share a soft blue outline and have warm amber model-badge pills below them; the other three (CLAIM, COMMIT, INTEGRATE) have thin grey outlines and a small grey 'harness action' label
Typography is clean editorial sans-serif, dark charcoal on white, no decorative effects
Connector arrows are thin solid grey chevrons; loop-back is a thin dashed grey curve
Absolutely no human figure, no character, no cyberpunk styling, no neon, no glow, no dark backgrounds — this is an editorial blog illustration
Situation.LightingFocus: Flat ambient — no dramatic lighting. Subtle soft drop-shadows under the rounded panels are the only depth cue.

ArticleContext.Title: Seven Coding Agents, One Space Invaders Clone
